channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [GOACARBON] Goa Carbon Ltd's Paradeep Unit Temporarily Shut Down for Maintenance
item.description:
Goa Carbon Limited has announced the temporary shut-down of its Paradeep Unit for scheduled maintenance work. The unit, located at Vill. Udayabata, Paradeepgarh, Dist. Jagatsinghpur, Odisha, has ceased operations from 22nd March 2026.

  • Goa Carbon Limited's Paradeep Unit is temporarily shut down
  • The shut-down is due to scheduled maintenance work
  • The unit is located at Vill. Udayabata, Paradeepgarh, Dist. Jagatsinghpur, Odisha
  • The maintenance work started from 22nd March 2026

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GOA CARBON LTD.-$ | Category: Business Update

item.title: [KIRLOSIND] Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Resumes Operations of High Pressure Moulding Line After Middle East Conflict Disruptions
item.description:
Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Limited (KFIL), a listed material subsidiary of Kirloskar Industries Ltd, has resumed the operations of one of the two High Pressure Moulding Lines situated at Solapur plant. The resumption follows the disruptions in global energy supply chains linked to the Middle East conflict which led to non-availability of Liquefied Petroleum Gas. KFIL has managed to resume the operations by using alternate fuel. The temporary stoppage of operations did not have any material financial impact.

  • Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Limited (KFIL) resumes operations of one High Pressure Moulding Line at Solapur plant.
  • Resumption follows disruptions in global energy supply chains linked to the Middle East conflict.
  • KFIL uses alternate fuel to resume operations.
  • Temporary stoppage of operations did not have any material financial impact.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Kirloskar Industries Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [MMFL] MM Forgings Ltd. to Raise Funds and Increase Authorized Share Capital
item.description:
MM Forgings Ltd. has announced the closure of its trading window in accordance with Regulation 29 of the SEBI Listing (Obligations and Disclosure Requirement) Regulations, 2015. The Board of Directors will meet on 25 March 2026 to consider and approve the raising of funds through Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP) or other eligible securities. The company also plans to increase its authorized share capital, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals. The trading window will reopen 48 hours after the conclusion of the Board Meeting. This information is hosted on the company's website at www.mmforgings.com.

  • MM Forgings Ltd. to raise funds through Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP) or other eligible securities
  • Company plans to increase its authorized share capital
  • Trading window closure with immediate effect
  • Trading window to reopen 48 hours after the Board Meeting
  • Approval of shareholders and regulatory authorities required

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: M.M.FORGINGS LTD.-$ | Category: Investment

item.title: [MMFL] MM Forgings Ltd. to Consider Funds Raising via QIP and Authorized Share Capital Increase
item.description:
MM Forgings Ltd. has announced that its Board of Directors will meet on 25 March 2026 to consider raising funds through the issuance of equity shares or other eligible securities, including Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP), subject to necessary approvals. The company will also seek shareholder approval for increasing the authorized share capital and altering the Memorandum of Association and/or Articles of Association. The Trading Window for dealing in the securities of the company will be closed with immediate effect, in accordance with the SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015.

  • MM Forgings Ltd. to consider raising funds through QIP
  • Company to increase authorized share capital
  • Shareholder approval sought for proposed changes
  • Trading Window closed in accordance with SEBI regulations

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: M.M.FORGINGS LTD.-$ | Category: Investment

item.title: [RITES] RITES Secures Major Order from RDSO for Comprehensive Maintenance Contract Worth Rs. 105.69 Crore
item.description:
🔥 Trending

RITES Ltd has been awarded a comprehensive maintenance contract by Research Designs and Standards Organisation (RDSO), Lucknow for a dedicated test track between Gudha and Thathana Mithri stations of Jodhpur division of NWR. The contract, valued at Rs. 105.69 Crore excluding GST, is to be executed over a period of 60 months from the date of issuance of LOA. This is a transaction between Government and Government Company, and does not fall under related party transactions.

  • RITES secures comprehensive maintenance contract from RDSO for dedicated test track
  • Contract worth Rs. 105.69 Crore excluding GST
  • To be executed over 60 months from LOA issuance
  • Transaction between Government and Government Company
  • Not a related party transaction

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: RITES Ltd | Category: Deals

item.title: [TATACAP] Tata Capital Ltd. Receives Income Tax Order for FY 2017-18 with Rs. 413.18 Crores Demand
item.description:
Tata Capital Limited has received an Income Tax Order under Section 143(3) read with Section 147 of the Income Tax Act, 1961, for Tata Capital Financial Services Limited (now merged with Tata Capital Limited) for the financial year 2017-18. The order raises a demand of Rs. 413.18 crores, primarily on account of short credit of taxes paid, interest levied and certain disallowances. The Company, however, does not envisage any material financial implication at this stage due to errors apparent from records in the Computation Sheet. Tata Capital Limited will take necessary steps to file a rectification application or appeal, and expects a favourable outcome in this matter.

  • Tata Capital Limited received an Income Tax Order under Section 143(3) read with Section 147 of the Income Tax Act, 1961, for Tata Capital Financial Services Limited for the financial year 2017-18.
  • The order raises a demand of Rs. 413.18 crores, primarily on account of short credit of taxes paid, interest levied and certain disallowances.
  • Tata Capital Limited does not foresee any material financial implications at this stage due to errors apparent from records in the Computation Sheet.
  • The Company will take necessary steps to file a rectification application or appeal and expects a favourable outcome in this matter.
  • Tata Capital Limited has filed or is in the process of filing an appeal with the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) for certain disallowances amounting to a tax impact of Rs. 26.31 crores for the Financial Year 2017-18.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Tata Capital Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [SOUTHBANK] South Indian Bank Ltd Appoints New Non-Executive Independent Director, Mr. Thomson Thomas
item.description:
South Indian Bank Ltd has appointed Mr. Thomson Thomas as an Additional Director (Non-Executive Independent Director) effective March 23, 2026. Mr. Thomas will replace Mr. V J Kurian, whose term as Independent Director will end on March 22, 2026. Mr. Thomas is a Chartered Accountant with 39 years of experience in Banking, Finance, Corporate Audit, Corporate Taxation, Risk Management, and Corporate Governance. He has been appointed for a term of three consecutive years, subject to the approval of the Bank's shareholders. Mr. Thomas is not related to any of the Bank's directors and is not debarred from holding the office of Director by any order of the Securities and Exchange Board of India or any other authority.

  • Mr. Thomson Thomas appointed as Non-Executive Independent Director
  • Replaces Mr. V J Kurian who will complete his term on March 22, 2026
  • Mr. Thomas has 39 years of experience in Banking, Finance, Corporate Audit, Corporate Taxation, Risk Management, and Corporate Governance
  • Appointed for a term of three consecutive years, subject to shareholders' approval
  • Not related to any of the Bank's directors and not debarred from holding the office of Director

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SOUTH INDIAN BANK LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SOUTHBANK] South Indian Bank Ltd Appoints Mr. Jose Joseph Kattoor as New Non-Executive Part Time Chairman
item.description:
South Indian Bank Ltd has announced the appointment of Mr. Jose Joseph Kattoor as the new Non-Executive Part Time Chairman (Independent Director) for a period of three years, effective from March 23, 2026. Mr. Kattoor, aged 62, brings extensive experience from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), where he served for over three decades and retired as an Executive Director in June 2023. He has held various key positions in RBI, including Enforcement Department, Corporate Strategy Department, Currency Department, and Human Resource Department. Mr. Kattoor is a graduate in Science, holds a Post Graduate Diploma in Rural Management from IRMA, a Law degree, is a Certified Associate of the Indian Institute of Bankers (CAIIB), and has completed the Advanced Management Program (AMP) from Wharton. He has been an Independent Director of the Bank since July 18, 2024. The appointment follows the retirement of the current Non-Executive Part Time Chairman, Mr. V J Kurian, upon completion of his term on March 22, 2026. The Bank has complied with the necessary regulations and disclosure requirements set by the SEBI and the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.

  • Appointment of Mr. Jose Joseph Kattoor as Non-Executive Part Time Chairman (Independent Director) for three years starting March 23, 2026.
  • Mr. Kattoor's extensive work experience in Reserve Bank of India (RBI) for over three decades.
  • Mr. Kattoor's academic credentials include B.Sc, PGDRM(IRMA), LLB, CAIIB, and AMP from Wharton.
  • Retirement of current Non-Executive Part Time Chairman, Mr. V J Kurian, on March 22, 2026.
  • Compliance with SEBI and Banking Regulation Act, 1949, regulations and disclosure requirements.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SOUTH INDIAN BANK LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SOUTHBANK] South Indian Bank Ltd: Mr. V J Kurian Retires, Mr. Jose Joseph Kattoor Appointed as New Non-Executive Part Time Chairman
item.description:
South Indian Bank Ltd announced that Mr. V J Kurian will retire from his position as Non-Executive Part Time Chairman (Independent Director) on March 22, 2026 upon completion of his term. Mr. Jose Joseph Kattoor will take over as the new Non-Executive Part Time Chairman (Independent Director) for a period of three years, effective from March 23, 2026. Mr. Kattoor has extensive experience in Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and has handled various departments during his tenure. He is also an Independent Director of the Bank since 18th July 2024. Mr. Kattoor is not related to any Directors of the Bank and is not debarred from holding the office of Director by any order of Securities and Exchange Board of India or any other such authority.

  • Mr. V J Kurian to retire as Non-Executive Part Time Chairman (Independent Director) of South Indian Bank Ltd on March 22, 2026.
  • Mr. Jose Joseph Kattoor to take charge as the new Non-Executive Part Time Chairman (Independent Director) for a period of three years, effective from March 23, 2026.
  • Mr. Kattoor has extensive experience in Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and has handled various departments during his tenure.
  • Mr. Kattoor is an Independent Director of the Bank since 18th July 2024.
  • Mr. Kattoor is not related to any Directors of the Bank and is not debarred from holding the office of Director by any order of Securities and Exchange Board of India or any other such authority.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SOUTH INDIAN BANK LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SUNCLAY] Sundaram-Clayton Ltd Acquires 39,047 Equity Shares of Navia Two Power Private Limited for Rs. 7,44,63,409
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Sundaram-Clayton Ltd has made an additional investment of Rs. 7,44,63,409 and acquired 39,047 equity shares of Navia Two Power Private Limited. The shares were acquired at a face value of Rs. 10/- each and at a premium of Rs. 1,897.02 per share. This acquisition increases the total shareholding with Navia from 11,628 equity shares to 50,675 equity shares. Navia is a special-purpose vehicle for the purpose of owning and operating a Captive Power Plant. The acquisition was made on 21st March 2026 at 09:00 p.m. (IST).

  • Sundaram-Clayton Ltd has acquired 39,047 equity shares of Navia Two Power Private Limited.
  • The investment amount is Rs. 7,44,63,409.
  • The shares were acquired at a face value of Rs. 10/- each and at a premium of Rs. 1,897.02 per share.
  • Navia is a special-purpose vehicle for the purpose of owning and operating a Captive Power Plant.
  • The acquisition increases the total shareholding with Navia from 11,628 equity shares to 50,675 equity shares.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sundaram-Clayton Ltd | Category: Acquisitions
